user_message: Pulling in context from Treyarch Global's escalation email -- need to draft a response for the QBR preparation.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 16:09 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 10:38 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 10:27 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Treyarch Global

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-J trap (Confuses planned maintenance with unplanned outage); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-J failure mode (Confuses planned maintenance with unplanned outage)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-J -- Confuses planned maintenance with unplanned outage
